assertion_results.text: Output imports BunkerSigner and parseBunkerInput from @nostr/tools/nip46 (or nostr-tools/nip46)
Output calls parseBunkerInput to parse the bunker:// URI
Output uses BunkerSigner.fromBunker() factory method (not constructor directly)
Output calls await bunker.connect() for the initial connection
Output calls await bunker.getPublicKey() after connecting and uses the returned value as the user identity
assertion_results.evidence: this.userPubkey = await this.signer.getPublicKey(); — called after connect(), stored as the user identity

assertion_results.text: Output includes an onauth callback in BunkerSignerParams that handles auth challenge URLs
assertion_results.evidence: onauth: (authUrl: string) => { const popup = window.open(authUrl, "nostr-auth", "width=600,height=700"); ... } — shown in the enhanced version with auth challenge support

assertion_results.text: Output demonstrates session persistence by storing BunkerPointer and client secret key, and shows reconnection without calling connect() again
assertion_results.evidence: Session persistence stores localSecretKeyHex and bunkerUri in localStorage. However, restoreSession() DOES call connect() again: 'await this.signer.connect();' — the assertion requires reconnection WITHOUT calling connect() again, which this output fails to do.

assertion_results.text: Output correctly distinguishes between remote-signer-pubkey (transport) and user-pubkey (identity)
assertion_results.evidence: IMPORTANT: The remote-signer-pubkey from the URI may differ from the user's pubkey. Always call getPublicKey() after connecting. Also: 'The pubkey in the bunker:// URI is the remote signer's pubkey, which may differ from the user's pubkey.'
